methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Deep Atomic is a pre-seed stage nuclear energy company founded by William G.J. Theron. The nuclear energy sector contains a modest comparable universe of 11 total firms—3 public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. Although 8 same-sector private peers exist, none satisfy narrow filtering criteria (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier), yielding zero qualified datapoints when a minimum of three is required for triangulation. A fallback to all-sector median would introduce unacceptable bias given the wide dispersion in maturity and capital intensity across the nuclear energy peer set.

The absence of sufficient pre-seed or early-stage nuclear comparables—combined with the capital-intensive and long-cycle nature of the sector—means traditional peer-based valuation is premature. A defensible valuation would require either: (1) progression to a later stage where staged-comparable density improves, or (2) adoption of non-comparable methods (DCF, venture capital method, or venture equity value methods) calibrated to the specific technical, regulatory, and financing risks inherent to pre-commercial nuclear projects. Without quantitative anchor points from the peer set, no valuation range can be supported.
